chief_complaint: A 75-year-old man presented with shoulder pain and weakness of the upper and lower extremities, worse on the right.
history: The patient has a history of hypertension, gout, type 2 diabetes, ileostomy from diverticulitis, and surgery one month prior for an intra-abdominal abscess. He initially presented with symptoms concerning for a stroke or acute coronary syndrome.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings included lateralizing extremity weakness.
labs: ECG: anteroseptal and inferior ST segment elevations. Other investigations: electrolyte panel, renal function tests, and imaging studies (not specified).
